id_requirements: Valid government-issued photo ID (passport or national identity card) required at all exam centres.
exam_rules: No electronic devices (phones, tablets, dictionaries) permitted in the exam room. Oral exams conducted face-to-face with a trained examiner. Bring your ID and registration confirmation.
renewal_options: All Goethe-Zertifikat certificates have lifetime validity — they do not expire.
target_audience: Young people (recommended ages 12 and above) and adults (recommended ages 16 and above)
recommended_experience: Basic language knowledge at the B1 level

prerequisites.description: None; recommended ~350 hours of German study. Widely required for German citizenship (since 2024) and university entrance for foreign applicants.

retake_policy.notes: No official limit on retakes. Candidates may re-sit at the next available exam date after a failed attempt.

objectives.title: Understand the main information if clear standard language is spoken and the conversation is about everyday topics like work, school or leisure
Understand uncomplicated non-fiction texts about their interests and their own field
Express themselves in a simple, structured and addressee-specific way about known topics, personal interests and experiences, dreams and goals, as well as give reasons and explanations
